text  
equilibrium population size is calculated by setting the derivative equal to zero and solving for P  
piece  
the equation becomes 0.1P(1 - P/1000) - 10 - 5sin(π/6t) = 0  
piece  
this leads to a quadratic equation in P which can be solved using standard methods  
highlighted section  
the solution to the equation gives the equilibrium population size P_e  
highlighted section  
the value of P_e is found to be 500 turtles when the harvesting function is constant  
text  
the stability of P_e is analyzed by examining the sign of the derivative around the equilibrium point  
piece  
if the derivative is negative, the equilibrium is stable; if positive, it is unstable  
piece  
the model predicts that the population will decline over time due to the decreasing carrying capacity  
text  
the time when the population will no longer be sustainable is when P_e(t) ≤ 0  
piece  
this occurs at t = 333 years when the carrying capacity reaches zero  
piece

text
